behavior: Often forages on the ground for ants and beetles; flies with deep flaps and glides, flashing the white rump and colored wing linings; excavates and uses tree cavities.
field_marks: Large, slim woodpecker with a rounded head, slightly downcurved pointed bill, and long tapered tail.
Brownish-gray upperparts finely barred black; pale underparts densely marked with round black spots.
Bold black crescent-shaped bib across the upper breast.
Bright white rump patch conspicuous in flight.
Underwings and underside of tail flash yellow or red, depending on form; males may show a black or red facial whisker.
habitat: Open woodlands, forest edges, scattered-tree fields, burned or disturbed woodland, parks, and other open habitats near trees; also uses wet woodland edges.
